title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gas in stomach
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gas in stomach are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs, which are taken orally to alleviate symptoms of gas and bloating. These tinctures typically contain herbs known for their carminative and anti-spasmodic properties, such as chamomile, ginger, peppermint, and fennel. These herbs work by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, reducing inflammation, and promoting the expulsion of gas from the digestive tract. By doing so, they can provide quick relief from discomfort and pain associated with gas in the stomach. For example, a person who frequently experiences gas and bloating after meals may find relief by taking a chamomile tincture before meals, allowing them to enjoy their meals without worrying about discomfort afterwards. This can significantly improve their quality of life, as they can go about their daily activities without the constant worry of gas and bloating.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tincture helps with gas in stomach because it contains volatile oils, including thujone, that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and bile in the liver, which in turn helps break down food and gases in the stomach more efficiently. Additionally, wormwood has antispasmodic properties that help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the likelihood of gas buildup and discomfort. Its carminative effects also promote the expulsion of excess gas from the digestive system, providing quick relief from bloating and flatulence. Overall, wormwood tincture offers a natural and effective solution for managing gas and promoting healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Wormwood tincture can help to expel gas from the stomach, providing relief from bloating and discomfort. This is due to the presence of volatile oils such as thujone and alpha-thujone.

Antispasmodic: Wormwood tincture can help to reduce muscle spasms in the digestive tract, which can help to prevent the formation of gas and alleviate pain. This is attributed to the presence of compounds like flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones.

Stomachic: Wormwood tincture can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes and improve overall digestion, which can help to reduce gas formation. The active constituents responsible for this include absinthin and anabsinthin.

Antimicrobial: Wormwood tincture can help to eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach that can contribute to the formation of gas. This is due to the presence of compounds such as thujone, artemisinin, and tannins.

Bitter Stimulant: Wormwood tincture can help to stimulate the production of digestive juices and bile, which can help to break down food and prevent the formation of gas. This is attributed to the presence of bitter compounds like absinthin and anabsinthin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Wormwood leaves are commonly used in medicinal teas for gas in the stomach due to their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to reduce bloating and flatulence.
Flowers: The flowers of the wormwood plant contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have been shown to aid digestion, alleviate spasms, and reduce gas accumulation in the gastrointestinal tract.

Seeds: Wormwood seeds are rich in essential oils and bitter compounds, making them an effective addition to teas for treating stomach gas by stimulating digestive function and reducing excess gas production.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried wormwood herb and place it in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the herb, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, discarding the spent herb, and transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, and store it in a cool, dark place or refrigerate.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it contains menthol, a compound that relaxes the gastrointestinal muscles and allows gas to pass through the digestive tract more easily. This can alleviate the discomfort and pain associated with gas buildup in the stomach. Additionally, peppermint has carminative properties, which means it can help to break up gas and reduce bloating. The tincture form of peppermint is also quickly absorbed by the body, making it a fast-acting remedy for gas and other digestive issues. Overall, peppermint tincture is a natural and effective way to relieve gas and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Peppermint tincture helps to expel gas from the stomach, providing relief from bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of menthol and methyl salicylate, which relax gastrointestinal muscles and allow gas to pass.

Antispasmodic: Peppermint tincture can help to reduce spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, which can cause gas buildup. This is because menthol, a key active constituent, acts as a natural muscle relaxant.

Analgesic: Peppermint tincture can help to alleviate pain associated with gas in the stomach. The presence of menthol and other volatile oils provides a cooling and soothing effect on the stomach lining, reducing pain and inflammation.

Antimicrobial: Peppermint tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate harmful bacteria in the stomach that can cause gas and other digestive issues. The active constituents responsible for this property include menthol, menthone, and limonene.

Choleretic: Peppermint tincture can help to stimulate the production of bile in the liver, which aids in the digestion of fats and can help to prevent gas buildup. This property is due to the presence of menthol, which stimulates the flow of bile from the gallbladder.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain menthol and menthone, which have antispasmodic properties that can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and alleviate gas and bloating.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and carminative effects that can help reduce gas and soothe the digestive system.
Seeds: Contain volatile oils and fatty acids that can help stimulate the digestive system, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of gas and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves, filling a clean, dry mason jar to ¾ full.
Pour 100-proof vodka over the leaves, ensuring they are completely submerged, leaving a 1-inch headspace.
Securely seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, gently shaking the jar every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean, amber glass bottle, discarding the spent peppermint leaves.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and dosage suggestions (e.g., 1-2 mL up to three times a day) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it contains volatile oils, such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole, which have carminative and antispasmodic properties. These compounds help to relax the gastrointestinal tract, reduce inflammation, and promote the expulsion of gas from the digestive system. Fennel also stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which can improve overall digestion and help to prevent the formation of gas in the first place. Its soothing effects on the stomach lining can provide quick relief from discomfort and pain caused by gas and bloating.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Fennel tincture can help to expel gas from the stomach, reducing feelings of bloating and discomfort. This property is important for gas in the stomach because it helps to relieve one of the main symptoms of this condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as anethole, fenchone, and estragole.

Antispasmodic: Fennel tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping. This property is important for gas in the stomach because it can help to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with gas buildup. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as anethole and fenchone.

Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to the development of gas. This property is important for gas in the stomach because it can help to prevent the buildup of gas in the first place. The active constituents responsible for this property include flavonoids such as rutin and quercetin.

Diuretic: Fennel tincture can help to promote the production of urine, which can help to remove excess water and gas from the body. This property is important for gas in the stomach because it can help to reduce bloating and discomfort. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as anethole and fenchone.

Digestive stimulant: Fennel tincture can help to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help to break down food and prevent the buildup of gas. This property is important for gas in the stomach because it can help to prevent the development of gas in the first place. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as anethole and fenchone.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Fennel seeds are commonly used as they contain oils with antispasmodic properties that can help relieve gas and bloating in the stomach.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have calming properties that can help soothe the digestive system, making it effective in reducing gas buildup.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the fennel plant contain compounds that stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, aiding in the breakdown of food and preventing the formation of gas in the stomach.
remedy_recipe: Chop one cup of fresh fennel leaves and seeds, place them in a clean jar, then add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80-100 proof).
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the solid plant material.
Transfer the liquid (tincture) to a clean, dark glass bottle or jar, sealing it tightly.
Label the bottle with the ingredients, date, and recommended dosage (typically 1-2 mL, 3 times daily) and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Caraway tincture helps with gas in stomach because it contains caraway oil, which has been traditionally used as a natural remedy for digestive issues. The oil's active components, such as carvone and limonene, can help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce bloating. Additionally, caraway oil can stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting the breakdown of food and preventing the formation of gas in the stomach. By alleviating symptoms of gas and bloating, caraway tincture can contribute to overall digestive health and promote a sense of well-being.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Caraway tincture helps to expel gas from the stomach, providing relief from bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvone and limonene.

Antispasmodic: Caraway tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can contribute to gas formation. This is attributed to the alkaloids found in caraway, such as saponins and flavonoids.

Digestive stimulant: Caraway tincture can help to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving the breakdown and absorption of food in the stomach, thereby reducing the formation of gas. This is due to the presence of essential oils, particularly carvone.

Antimicrobial: Caraway tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the stomach, reducing the production of gas. This is due to the presence of phenolic compounds, such as gallic acid and caffeic acid.

Anti-inflammatory: Caraway tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, which can contribute to the formation of gas. This is due to the presence of flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Caraway seeds are commonly used in making medicinal tea for gas in the stomach due to their carminative properties that help to relieve bloating and flatulence.
Fruits: The dried ripe fruits of caraway are often used interchangeably with seeds and contain essential oils that aid in digestion and alleviate symptoms of gas and stomach discomfort.

Leaves: Caraway leaves, although less commonly used, can be steeped in hot water to create a tea that helps soothe digestive issues, as they contain antioxidants and volatile oils beneficial for gut health.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried caraway seeds into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum (80 proof) over the chopped seeds until they are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out as much liquid as possible.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle in a cool, dark place, and take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for gas relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it has anti-spasmodic and carminative properties that aid in reducing bloating and gas. It works by relaxing the gastrointestinal muscles, which allows for the release of trapped gas and prevents the formation of new gas. Additionally, lemon balm has calming effects on the digestive system, which can help alleviate discomfort caused by gas. Its soothing properties can also help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further aiding in the relief of gas and bloating.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Lemon balm tincture helps to relieve gas and bloating in the stomach by promoting the expulsion of intestinal gas due to its volatile oils, including citral and citronellal.
Spasmolytic: Its antispasmodic properties, attributed to eugenol and rosmarinic acid, help reduce muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating discomfort caused by gas.

Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tincture can help manage stress-induced gastric issues, as its rosmarinic acid and flavonoids content contribute to anxiety reduction, which can help prevent gas buildup in the stomach.

Antimicrobial: Lemon balm tincture's antimicrobial properties, owing to its terpenes and phenolic acids, can help inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gut that may cause gas and other digestive issues.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory effects of lemon balm tincture, due to its rosmarinic acid and eugenol content, can help soothe inflamed gastrointestinal tissues, reducing irritation and gas production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm are commonly used in making medicinal tea for gas in the stomach due to their antispasmodic and carminative properties, which help to relieve bloating and flatulence.

Flowers: The fragrant flowers of lemon balm contain volatile oils and flavonoids that have a calming effect on the digestive system, making them useful in treating gas and other digestive discomforts.
Stems: While not as potent as the leaves or flowers, the stems of lemon balm still contain some of the same beneficial compounds and can be included in tea blends to aid in digestion and reduce gas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh lemon balm leaves, gently bruising them to release their oils.
Place the leaves in a clean jar, adding 1 cup of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) to cover the leaves.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the leaves.
Store the tincture in a tightly sealed glass bottle, taking 1-2 ml up to three times a day for gas relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garden angelica tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it promotes the production of digestive juices, which can help break down food and reduce the formation of gas. Additionally, it has carminative properties, meaning it can help to expel gas from the digestive tract, providing relief from discomfort and bloating. Angelica tincture may also help to soothe and relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing muscle spasms that can contribute to the formation of gas. Its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, further alleviating symptoms of gas and bloating. Overall, angelica tincture can be a helpful natural remedy for addressing digestive issues related to gas.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Garden angelica tincture can help to expel gas from the stomach, providing relief from bloating and discomfort. This property is important for gas in the stomach because it helps to alleviate one of the main symptoms of the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include volatile oils such as alpha-pinene and limonene.

Antispasmodic: The tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing spasms and cramping. This is important for gas in the stomach because spasms and cramping can exacerbate the condition and cause pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include the coumarin derivatives and the essential oil.

Anti-inflammatory: Garden angelica tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract. This is important for gas in the stomach because inflammation can contribute to the development and persistence of the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include the essential oil, particularly the sesquiterpenes, and the coumarin derivatives.

Diuretic: The tincture can help to increase urine production, which can help to remove excess fluids and waste products from the body. remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of garden angelica contain compounds that can help reduce gas and bloating in the stomach.
Seeds: The seeds of garden angelica are often used in medicinal teas to help soothe digestive issues, including gas and bloating.
Stems: The stems of garden angelica contain essential oils that can help relax the gastrointestinal tract and reduce gas buildup.
Leaves: The leaves of garden angelica are rich in antioxidants and can help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating gas and bloating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh Angelica plant roots, rinse, and chop into small pieces (preferably during the morning of a sunny day).
Fill a clean glass jar with chopped Angelica roots, then add 80-proof vodka until the roots are completely submerged (use 1:2 ratio, e.g., 2 cups of chopped roots to 4 cups of vodka).
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for about 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the maceration period, strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Transfer the strained liquid (tincture) to a dark glass bottle, seal it, and store in a cool, dark place; take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for gas relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it has carminative properties, which means it can help to relieve flatulence and gastric discomfort. Spearmint contains volatile oils, such as carvone and limonene, which have antispasmodic effects on the gastrointestinal tract, helping to reduce muscle spasms and relax the stomach, thereby alleviating the symptoms of gas and bloating. Additionally, spearmint has been shown to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, which may help to eliminate the overgrowth of harmful bacteria in the gut that can contribute to the development of gas and other digestive issues. Overall, spearmint tincture can provide a natural and effective way to relieve symptoms of gas and promote healthy digestion.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Spearmint tincture helps to expel gas from the stomach, providing relief from bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of menthol and carvone, which relax the gastrointestinal tract and allow for the easy expulsion of gas.

Antispasmodic: Spearmint tincture helps to reduce smooth muscle spasms in the gastrointestinal tract, alleviating symptoms of gas and cramping. This is attributed to the presence of menthol, which has antispasmodic effects on smooth muscle.

Anti-inflammatory: Spearmint tincture helps to reduce inflammation in the gastrointestinal tract, which can contribute to the formation of gas. This is due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties.

Antimicrobial: Spearmint tincture helps to inhibit the growth of harmful bacteria in the gastrointestinal tract, reducing the production of gas. This is due to the presence of menthol and carvone, which have antimicrobial properties.

Digestive stimulant: Spearmint tincture helps to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, promoting the breakdown of food and reducing the formation of gas. This is due to the presence of menthol, which has a stimulating effect on the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the spearmint plant are commonly used in medicinal teas for gas in the stomach due to their carminative properties, which help to relax the gastrointestinal tract and alleviate symptoms of bloating and gas.
Flowers: The flowers of the spearmint plant contain volatile oils that have antispasmodic and carminative effects, making them useful for reducing gas and promoting digestion in medicinal teas.
Seeds: Spearmint seeds are rich in essential oils and have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, making them a valuable addition to medicinal teas for gas in the stomach.

Stems: The stems of the spearmint plant contain therapeutic compounds that can help to relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce symptoms of gas and bloating, making them a common ingredient in medicinal teas.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of fresh spearmint leaves and stems, placing them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, discarding the plant material.
Store the resulting spearmint tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1/4 teaspoon as needed for gas relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it contains carvacrol and thymol, two compounds known for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. These components help relax the gastrointestinal tract, reducing muscle spasms and alleviating the buildup of gas. Additionally, oregano has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help eliminate harmful bacteria and yeast in the gut, which can contribute to digestive issues like gas and bloating. By promoting the balance of gut flora and soothing the digestive tract, oregano tincture can effectively provide relief from gas and improve overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Carminative: Oregano tincture can help to expel gas from the stomach and intestines, providing relief from bloating and discomfort. This property is due to the presence of volatile oils such as carvacrol and thymol.

Antispasmodic: Oregano tincture can help to relax the smooth muscles of the gastrointestinal tract, reducing cramping and spasms that can contribute to gas build-up. This property is attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids, terpenes, and sterols.

remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the oregano tincture plant are used to make medicinal tea for gas in the stomach due to their carminative properties, which help to relieve bloating and flatulence.

Flowers: The flowers of the oregano tincture plant are used in medicinal tea for gas in the stomach as they contain volatile oils that have antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe and relax the digestive tract.

remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh oregano leaves and stems, placing them in a clean jar until it's 3/4 full.
Pour vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the herbs until they are completely submerged, filling the jar to the top.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid.
Store the final tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, labeling it with the ingredients, date, and potency. Take 1-2 mL up to three times a day as needed for gas relief.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thyme leaves, filling a clean, dry mason jar halfway, then add 80-proof vodka until the plant material is fully submerged.
Tightly seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place, gently shaking it once a day for two to four weeks.
After the allotted time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the plant material.
Transfer the resulting thyme tincture to a clean, dark glass bottle or jar, sealing it tightly to preserve the potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tincture helps with gas in the stomach because it contains active compounds called gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to help relax the gastrointestinal muscles and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. This can help alleviate symptoms of gas, bloating, and discomfort. Ginger also stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which can help break down food more efficiently and prevent the formation of gas in the first place. remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Ginger rhizomes are widely used to make medicinal tea for gas in stomach due to their potent carminative properties that help to relieve bloating and flatulence.

Seeds: Ginger seeds contain volatile oils and other compounds that have been shown to aid in digestion and reduce gas and bloating, making them a valuable addition to medicinal tea for stomach ailments.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh ginger root into small pieces and place in a clean jar.
Add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to the jar, ensuring the ginger is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the specified time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the liquid and discarding the solids.
Store the resulting tincture in a tightly sealed glass container in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Take 1-2 mL (1/4-1/2 teaspoon) as needed for gas relief.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gas in stomach with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of fennel, ginger, and peppermint tinctures. Fennel aids in digestion and reduces bloating, while ginger alleviates pain and inflammation. Peppermint, on the other hand, relaxes gastrointestinal muscles, allowing gas to pass through more easily. Mix 10-15 drops of each tincture in a glass of water and drink it after meals. This natural remedy provides fast-acting relief and promotes overall digestive health.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gas in stomach that are treated with herbal tinctures include bloating, indigestion, and constipation. For instance, peppermint tincture is often used to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, such as bloating and gas. Ginger tincture can help reduce inflammation and nausea, while chamomile tincture has antispasmodic properties that can soothe the digestive tract and relieve cramping and gas. Dandelion tincture can stimulate digestion and promote the flow of bile, which can help prevent gas and bloating. It is essential to consult with a healthcare provider before taking any herbal tinctures to ensure safety and effectiveness.
